tips: Remove shoes before entering prayer halls
Walk clockwise around monasteries, stupas, and mani walls
Do not touch religious artifacts, murals, or thangka paintings
Ask permission before photographing monks or ceremonies
Dress modestly — cover shoulders and knees
Speak softly inside prayer halls
Do not sit on raised platforms meant for monks or lamas
Leave a small donation if you wish (₹10-50 is fine)
Do not smoke or eat inside monastery premises
Greet with 'Julley' (means hello, thank you, and goodbye in Ladakhi)
Accept tea or food offerings with both hands
Don't point your feet toward people or religious objects
Don't step on or remove prayer flags
Respect the environment — Ladakh's ecosystem is extremely fragile
Carry reusable water bottles — plastic waste is a serious problem
Don't buy fossils, artifacts, or animal products — it's illegal
Ask before photographing locals, especially elders
Tipping is appreciated but not expected. ₹50-100 for good service.
Learn basic Ladakhi: 'Juk' (sit), 'Khamzang inna?' (How are you?), 'Thuk-je che' (Thank you)
Hire local guides and porters — supports the local economy
Don't leave trash on trails. Pack out everything you pack in.
Don't disturb wildlife or stray from marked trails
Respect private land and farming areas
Use designated camping sites when available
Don't light fires in sensitive areas — use stoves
Chadar Trek should only be attempted with experienced local operators
Carry your own first aid kit and know basic AMS treatment
Always ask before photographing people, especially monks and elders
Some monasteries charge a camera fee (₹20-50)
Drone photography requires permits and is restricted near military areas
Don't photograph military installations, bridges, or border areas
Be respectful during religious ceremonies — don't use flash
Offer to share photos with locals you photograph
Carry reusable water bottles — refill at cafes and homestays
Say no to single-use plastic bags
Don't buy bottled water if you can avoid it
Use biodegradable toiletries
Don't pick wildflowers or disturb wildlife
Support eco-friendly accommodations and tour operators
Carry a small trash bag on treks and collect any litter you see
Use shared transport when possible to reduce carbon footprint
